metaDescription: How to rename, reorganize, and merge accounts in QuickBooks Online's Chart of Accounts without breaking your existing balance sheet.
intro: A messy Chart of Accounts doesn't have to mean starting over. QuickBooks Online lets you rename categories, restructure sub-accounts, and even merge duplicate entries while keeping your historical transactions intact. This guide covers editing both the Chart of Accounts and your sales-facing Products and Services categories.

steps.heading: Open the Chart of Accounts
steps.body: From the left sidebar, click Accounting, then Chart of Accounts to see your full ledger category list.

steps.heading: Edit a specific account
steps.body: Find the account you want to change, click the dropdown arrow on the right of its row, and select Edit.

steps.heading: Update name and hierarchy
steps.body: Change the account name, adjust its detail type, and check or uncheck Is Sub-Account to restructure its position in your hierarchy.

steps.heading: Merge duplicate accounts
steps.body: To merge two accounts, edit one so its name exactly matches the other, then confirm the merge prompt to consolidate all historical transactions.

steps.heading: Manage sales categories separately
steps.body: For customer-facing groups, go to the Gear icon and Products and Services to rename or batch-move items into new tracking families.

faq.question: Will renaming an account affect past transactions?
faq.answer: No, renaming updates the label going forward while preserving all historical transactions tied to that account.

faq.question: How do I merge two duplicate accounts?
faq.answer: Edit one account so its name exactly matches the other, then confirm the merge prompt to combine both accounts' transaction histories.

faq.question: Can I convert a standalone account into a sub-account?
faq.answer: Yes, checking the Is Sub-Account box during editing lets you nest it under a parent account and restructure your hierarchy.

faq.question: Is editing Chart of Accounts categories different from editing Products and Services?
faq.answer: Yes, Chart of Accounts controls your internal ledger categories, while Products and Services manages the customer-facing sales items you invoice against.
